titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Day Family, Photographs Related to Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians, and Sites in Arizona and New Mexico
Day Family, Photographs Related to Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians, and Sites in Arizona and New Mexico
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 6, 2020 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Photographs Related to Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians, and Sites in Arizona and New Mexico

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photographs collected by the Day family and leaves from a disbound photograph album compiled by them that chiefly relate to their trade and activities with Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians in Arizona and New Mexico, 1900-1941, as well as views of sites in Arizona, including Canyon de Chelly, St. Michael's Mission, and Walpi, and New Mexico including Gallup, Laguna Pueblo, and Las Vegas. Members of the Day family and Simeon Schwemberger created many of the photographs. Events documented include a foot race at Gallup, New Mexico, on July 4, 1908, a visit by Theodore Roosevelt to Walpi, Arizona, in August 1913, and the dedication ceremony for the El Navajo Hotel at Gallup, New Mexico, in May 1923. The collection includes portraits of Day family members and their acquaintances, including Walter Charles Beddow, Natalie Curtis Burlin, Stewart Culin, Berard Haile, Frederick Monsen, Simeon Schwemberger, and Anselm Weber, as well as still lifes, printed material, and picture postcards.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Andrew Smith Gallery on the Walter McClintock Fund, 2019.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Photograph Album Leaves, approximately 1905. II. Photographs, 1900-1941. III. Printed Material, approximately 1910-approximately 1937.

bioghist.head: Day Family
bioghist.p: The Day family were traders at the Navajo Indian Reservation in eastern Arizona. In 1883, Samuel Edward Day, Sr. (1845-1925) surveyed extensions to eastern and southern boundaries of the Navajo reservation in Arizona Territory for the United States government. Soon afterward, his wife, Anna P. Burbridge Day (1849-1932), and their sons, Charles L. Day (1879-1918), Samuel Edward Day, Jr. (1882-1963), and William Day (born 1885), joined him at a homestead at what became Window Rock, Arizona, and was also the site of the St. Michael's Mission founded in 1896 by the Order of the Friars Minor (Franciscans). In 1902, the Day family established the Thunderbird Trading Post at mouth of Canyon de Chelly, Arizona. In 1912, Samuel Edward Day, Jr. married married Kate Roanhorse (1885-1960), a daughter of Navajo chief Manuelito (1818-1893).

bioghist.head: Simeon Schwemberger (1867-1931)
bioghist.p: Simeon Schwemberger, born George C. Schwemberger, was a Franciscan friar, photographer, and trader in Arizona and New Mexico. Born in Cincinnati, Ohio, he entered candidacy into the Order of the Friars Minor (Franciscans) in 1887 and took solemn vows in 1896. In 1901, he joined the St. Michael's Mission at Window Rock, Arizona, and the following year began experimenting with photography. In 1908, Schwemberger left the Mission and Order and operated the Indian Arts Studio at Gallup, New Mexico. From 1911 until his death, he worked at trading posts at Gallup and Cedar Springs, New Mexico.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Day Family, Photographs Related to Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians, and Sites in Arizona and New Mexico. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid was produced from a preliminary item-level list provided by the dealer and augmented by the processing archivist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs collected by the Day family and leaves from a disbound photograph album compiled by them that chiefly relate to their trade and activities with Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians in Arizona and New Mexico, 1900-1941, as well as views of sites in Arizona, including Canyon de Chelly, St. Michael's Mission, and Walpi, and New Mexico including Gallup, Laguna Pueblo, and Las Vegas. Members of the Day family and Simeon Schwemberger created many of the photographs. Events documented include a foot race at Gallup, New Mexico, on July 4, 1908, a visit by Theodore Roosevelt to Walpi, Arizona, in August 1913, and the dedication ceremony for the El Navajo Hotel at Gallup, New Mexico, in May 1923. The collection includes portraits of Day family members and their acquaintances, including Walter Charles Beddow, Natalie Curtis Burlin, Stewart Culin, Berard Haile, Frederick Monsen, Simeon Schwemberger, and Anselm Weber, as well as still lifes, printed material, and picture postcards.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Day Family, Photographs Related to Navajo, Havasupai, Hopi, and Pueblo Indians, and Sites in Arizona and New Mexico, is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Photograph Album Leaves

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leaves from a photograph album compiled by the Day family, approximately 1905.

did.unittitle: Exterior portrait of a Hopi woman and child at a window of a dwelling surrounded by dried corn, baskets, and pottery
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 8.6 x 11.7 cm, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.3 cm

did.unittitle: Exterior portrait of a Navajo man on horseback
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 12 x 9.6 cm, mounted on 2 leaves, 17.4 x 23.3 cm, and 1 photographic print, 9.9 x 8.8 cm

did.unittitle: Exterior portraits including a Navajo woman with two Navajo children by a cliff with petroglyph as well as a Navajo woman working at a loom
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 11.8 x 9.4 cm and smaller, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.1 cm, and 1 photographic print, 11.7 x 9.2 cm

did.unittitle: Exterior portraits including three Havasupai children, identified as school children, as well as a woman with a "Kathak" basket and infant, and a view of two women a Havasupai dwelling
did.physdesc: 3 photographic prints, 9 x 10.9 cm and smaller, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.5 cm

did.unittitle: Exterior portraits of Havasupai Indians and dwellings including discrete portraits of Havasupai women wearing "Kathak" baskets
did.physdesc: 4 photographic prints, 8.5 x 9 cm and smaller, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.6 cm

did.unittitle: Exterior portraits of Havasupai Indians near dwellings including Captain Bill and his wife as well as portraits of Havasupai women
did.physdesc: 4 photographic prints, 8.3 x 11.7 cm and smaller, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.4 cm

did.unittitle: Interior portrait of a Navajo man
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 11.6 x 9.3 cm, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.2 cm

did.unittitle: View of a canyon
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 7.5 x 9.9 cm, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.5 cm

did.unittitle: View of a rock outcropping
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 9.1 x 11.8 cm, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.5 cm

did.unittitle: View of Frederick Monsen at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona, as well as a view at the site
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 12.9 x 10.2 cm, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 22.3 cm

did.unittitle: View of three Havasupai and a child trading baskets for calico fabric with a white woman as well as a view a camp at Supai, Arizona
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 9.3 x 11.1 cm and smaller, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.4 cm

did.unittitle: View of three Navajo women and a Navajo child on horseback at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 9 x 11.7 cm, mounted on leaf, 17.4 x 23.2 cm

did.unittitle: Photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Activities and Events, Portraits, Views, and Still Lifes

did.unittitle: Activities and Events
Butterfly Dance at First Mesa, Arizona
Dedication ceremony for the El Navajo Hotel, Gallup, New Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Kate Roanhorse Day and Samuel Edward Day, Jr., standing on the buckskin as part of a Blessingway ceremony

did.unittitle: Fire Dance performers carrying bundles at the property of Samuel Edward Day at Chinle, Arizona
Gallup, New Mexico, following a flood
Hopi boy, nude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph by Simeon Schwemberger; formerly housed in envelope from the Indian Art Studio, Gallup, New Mexico

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: For the envelope see: Series III. Printed Material, Box 2, folder 95

did.unittitle: Hopi boys, swimming nude

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph by Simeon Schwemberger; formerly housed in envelope from the Indian Art Studio, Gallup, New Mexico

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: For the envelope see: Series III. Printed Material, Box 2, folder 95

did.unittitle: Hopi children playing in water at Walpi, Arizona
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 7.9 x 10.4 cm, on mount, 13.9 x 19.3 cm

did.unittitle: Interior with two men, one man wearing a mask and regalia, and a sand painting
Medicine lodge frame construction
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 11.8 x 16.8 cm and smaller

did.unittitle: Navajo ceremonies with Yébîchai dancers
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 15.9 x 21.1 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a depiction of Gaaskidi, Zahadolzha, Haschelti; photographs by Edward S. Curtis; one print has water damage

did.unittitle: Navajo man on horseback carrying wood at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 11.6 x 16.8 cm, on mount, 17.8 x 22.8

did.unittitle: Navajo men on horseback and participating in a "chicken pull" competition as well as spectators, probably at the Hubbell Trading Post, Ganado, Arizona
did.physdesc: 6 photographic prints, 12.9 x 11.7 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A "chicken pull" is a horse riding competition: a rider gallops their horse at full speed toward a "chicken" (usually a small burlap bag filled with coins) partially buried in a sandy ground, which a rider snatches and then protects from other riders. Includes photograph by Simeon Schwemberger

did.unittitle: Navajo man tending a fire and heating rocks in front of a sweat lodge
Navajo women and men in a line during a medicine ceremony at Chinle, Arizona
Nine men in a camp, preparing a meal
Samuel Edward Day, Jr., and another man, nude and bathing in a body of water

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph by Simeon Schwemberger; formerly housed in envelope from the Indian Art Studio, Gallup, New Mexico

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated material: For the envelope see: Series III. Printed Material, Box 2, folder 95

did.unittitle: Samuel Edward Day, Jr., running a foot race with spectators at Gallup, New Mexico
Sand painting during a Female Shooting Chant Ceremony with a Navajo child and woman
Tablita Dance, also known as Corn Dance, performers at a pueblo, probably at New Mexico
Theodore Roosevelt, Natalie Curtis Burlin, and party at a Snake Dance at Dance Rock, Walpi, Arizona
did.physdesc: 20 photographic prints, 10 x 12.7 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images document Roosevelt and others in the audience, including photographers with cameras, as well as the dancers

did.unittitle: Three men, likely branding a steer, with two horses nearby, in the vicinity of the Indian Wells Trading Post, Winslow, Arizona
Two Navajo men gathering wood with a horse drawn wagon
Portraits
Anselm Weber and four men in front of St. Michael's Mission at Window Rock, Arizona
Anselm Weber on horseback at St. Michael's Mission at Window Rock, Arizona
Anselm Weber, probably at St. Michael's Mission at Window Rock, Arizona
Bonifacio Baca, Samuel Edward Day, Jr., and a Navajo man, wearing leg shackles, likely a prisoner
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 16.1 x 12.1 cm, on mount, 22.8 x 17.9

did.unittitle: Charles L. Day wearing regalia as a God Impersonator, including a headdress, body paint, skirt, medicine bag, and a concha belt, for the corral and fire dances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph possibly by Samuel Edward Day, Jr.; ink inscription on verso

did.unittitle: Charles P. Gage, forest ranger at Datil National Forest, New Mexico
Day family and others at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
did.physdesc: 2 photographic postcards, 8.9 x 14 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A photograph by Simeon Schwemberger captures a group portrait that includes Grace Tibbits, Grace Smith, Lilian, Anna P. Burbridge Day, Ruth, and Samuel Edward Day, Sr.; a photograph by an unidentified photographer includes Walter Charles Beddow, Clinton N. Cotton, and Samuel Edward Day, Jr.

did.unittitle: Day family and their homestead at Window Rock, Arizona
did.physdesc: 3 photographic postcards, 8.8 x 14 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a group portraits with Anna P. Burbridge Day, Samuel Edward Day, Sr., and Emma Burbridge Crosser

did.unittitle: Day family dog
Election Board at St. Michael's Mission at Window Rock, Arizona

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Group portrait, from left to right, Samuel Edward Day, Jr., Berard Haile, Simeon Schwemberger, Anselm Weber, and Daniel H. Mitchell

did.unittitle: Grace Tibbets Day and another woman on a wooden platform
Group portraits on return trip from the Snake Dance at Walpi, Arizona
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 8.5 x 8. 5 cm, on mounts 12.5 x 12.5 cm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Persons in the portraits include Anna P. Burbridge Day, Charlie L. Day, Samuel Edward Day, Sr., William A. Day, Edward Dempsey, Dr. Henry, Agnes Marshall Maupin, Leopold Osterman, and Simeon Schwemberger; photographs by Sally Washington Maupin

did.unittitle: Halalby Neznubegay family, near Peach Springs, New Mexico
Hatahle Yazza family including four women and five children
Hatahle Yazza with masks
Hopi woman gathering wood
Hosteen Yazza and a large group of Navajo men on horseback, probably at a "chicken pull" competition
Navajo family of four children, one woman, and two men by a newly-constructed hogan at Salina, Arizona
Navajo man, identified as "Holol Yaz," with two women, a girl, and two children
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 9.3 x 11.9 cm, on mount, 13.5 x 16

did.unittitle: Navajo man on horseback
Navajo man on horseback at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
Navajo man sitting and holding a basket with feather bundles
Navajo men
did.physdesc: 3 photographic prints, 17.6 x 12.6 cm and smaller on mounts, 20.2 x 15.3 and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discrete portraits of Navajo men including Hasteen Yazzie, an unidentified shaman, and a man about to participate in the Wind Chant ceremony; photographs by Charles L. Day

did.unittitle: Navajo men, five men posed sitting on a bench including Hasteen Benally and "Johnnie"
Navajo woman and a child holding a bow and arrows at Canyon del Muerto, a branch of Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 9.7 x 11.9 cm, on mount, 13.5 x 16

did.unittitle: Pueblo Indians, girl and boy
Red Beard (Dághaa' Łichíí) and Mr. Arm (Hastin Aconi), Navajo men
Samuel Edward Day, Jr., wearing chaps and holding a lasso
Simeon Schwemberger, self-portrait
Simeon Schwemberger holding a shotgun with dead rabbits
Stewart Culin with Navajo blankets and animal pelt as well as holding a rifle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stewart Culin (1858-1929) was an ethnographer interested in games, art, and dress; photograph by Simeon Schwemberger

did.unittitle: Two men with a wagon loaded with furniture
Water Charles Beddow and Charles Day wearing women's clothing from behind
Water Charles Beddow standing on a dirt road at Gallup, New Mexico
White Singer and Hosteen Bezadie posed standing with Shooting Chant Symbols at their feet
Zuni Dick, also known as Tsinahe/Tsanahe/Tsnahey, holding a rifle with his wife
Views
Agathla Peak, Rainbow Bridge, and other rock outcroppings, Arizona and Utah
did.physdesc: 6 photographic prints, 12.6 x 17.6 cm and smaller

did.unittitle: Battleship fleet from shore
Burro paddock, windmill, and water tanks with a Navajo man
Cedar Springs, Arizona
Chinle, Arizona
did.physdesc: 2 photographic postcard, 8.9 x 13.9 cm and smaller

did.unittitle: Cliff base and wash at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
Cliff dwellings, including the White House Ruin, and corn field at Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 11.6 x 17 cm, on mount, 17.8 x 22.8, and 3 photographic prints, 10.1 x 12.1 cm and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mounted photograph by Charles L. Day

did.unittitle: Cliff face with petroglyphs of two animal figures
did.physdesc: 1 photographic print, 11.4 x 16.9 cm, on mount, 12.6 x 17.6

did.unittitle: Dance Rock at Walpi, Arizona
did.physdesc: 2 photographic prints, 21.5 x 16.9 cm and smaller

did.unittitle: Gallup, New Mexico, goat herd at a ranch
Inn between Navajo lands and Adamana, Arizona
Laguna Pueblo, New Mexico
Las Vegas, New Mexico, aerial view

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph by Three Hawks Aero Foto, Minneapolis, Minnesota

did.unittitle: Masks inside Shrine Cave, east of Round Rock, Arizona
Navajo man and a pack mule as well as two women in a wooded terrain
Oil well with a natural gas flare at Seven Lakes, New Mexico
Oraibi, Arizona
Residence
Stream through a meadow and forest
Thunderbird Trading Post, Chinle, Arizona
Trading post, probably the Thunderbird Trading Post, Chinle, Arizona, with a group of men on horseback
Walpi, Arizona
Still Lifes
Basket
Painting of Frederick Monsen

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Painting dated 1929; copy photograph by Louis Henry Dreyer

did.unittitle: Printed Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed material collected by the Day family, approximately 1910-approximately 1937.

did.unittitle: Envelope from the Indian Art Studio, Gallup, New Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Indian Art Studio was operated by Simeon Schwemberger at Gallup, New Mexico, 1908-1911. It served as his photographic studio as well as selling Navajo crafts. The envelope has inscription, "not for ladys to look at," which reference photographs in the collection of nude children and men

title.part: Indian Men in Ceremonial Dance Costume, New Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscription on verso from "Harry" to Samuel Edward Day, III, August 1937

did.unittitle: New Mexico sites including Acoma Pueblo and the New Mexico Museum of Art at Santa Fe
did.physdesc: 2 relief halftones, 6.3 x 11.1 cm and smaller

did.unittitle: Tearsheet with six views of sites at Canyon del Muerto, a branch of Canyon de Chelly, Arizona
did.physdesc: 1 tear sheet, 23.6 x 15.9 cm, with 6 relief halftones, 4.8 x 6.1 cm

title.part: White House in the Rocks, Canon de Chelly, Ariz.
A Winter Scene at the United States Indian School, Toadlena, N. Mex.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscription on verso by James D. Kennedy, December 1930
